arts.ttl: Spot-on Chelsea win group after Macario’s double sees off Real Madrid
arts.athr: Sid Lowe at the Estadio Alfredo di Stéfano
arts.sum: For much of a cold night Chelsea were under pressure, taken to the line by Real Madrid, but ten minutes of Catarina Macario was enough to maintain their unbeaten record and take them through as winners of Group B. Six meetings later and still this is a fixture the Spanish club can’t win but they may never have been closer. Leading thanks to a goal from the immensely impressive Caroline Weir, they were eventually defeated by the American – who came on at half-time and belted in two penalties. In the end that was enough, Madrid pushing to the last but unable to find a way through that they might have felt they deserved after an early goal that came as a statement of intent in the opening period where Chelsea never managed to get control, Erin Cuthbert often a lone figure surrounded by white. Madrid kept possession, letting their opponents see the ball but not touch it, and although it was worked all the way back to their goalkeeper, Misa Rodríguez, it was not a move to nowhere. Instead, it served as the starting point for a sequence of twelve more passes that ended with it in the net at the other end. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Official: Real Madrid clean up with ‘The Best’ Player & Manager awards
arts.sum: La Liga giants Real Madrid on Tuesday saw the excellence of leading members of the club’s playing and coaching staff alike recognised. As much came by way of ‘The Best’…

arts.ttl: Vinicius Junior wins FIFA 'The Best Award' - Boycotted Ballon d'Or in October
arts.sum: Real Madrid star Vinicius Junior has been voted the best footballer in the world at a FIFA event in Doha. The Brazilian succeeds Lionel Messi, who was also nominated for the event. He...

arts.ttl: Immediate Reaction: Real Madrid 1-2 Chelsea; UWCL 2024
arts.sum: In the second minute of the encounter, Weir shot on target from the entrance of the penalty area after a recovery by Angeldahl at the touchline on the right. From the start of the second half, Macario went on to attack.

arts.ttl: Vinicius and Real Madrid dominate FIFA's The Best Awards
arts.sum: A fter the disappointment of losing out in the Ballon d'Or voting, Real Madrid's Brazilian striker Vinicius Jr. was named The Best FIFA Men's Player at the awards ceremony held in Doha, Qatar. "I want to share this with the club, with my president and with my players, those of the best club in the world," Ancelotti said on stage as he received his award.

arts.ttl: FIFA Best Awards: World's top XI revealed as stars from Barcelona, Real Madrid, Man City and Aston Villa all earn spots
arts.sum: After votes from fans and experts football's governing body has revealed the best XI of 2024 at a glamorous awards ceremony in Qatar

arts.ttl: Vinicius Junior named The Best FIFA Men's Player award for 2024
arts.sum: Real Madrid star Vinicius Junior has been named 2024's winner of The Best FIFA Men's Player prize.

arts.ttl: BREAKING: Vinicius Junior wins FIFA Best award after Real Madrid star's Ballon d'Or fury
arts.sum: Rodri beat Real Madrid star Vinicius Junior to win the Ballon d'Or in October but the Brazilian has got his own back by pipping the Manchester City midfielder to the men's FIFA Best award

arts.ttl: Carlo Ancelotti scoops The Best FIFA Men's Coach award for 2024
arts.sum: Real Madrid manager Carlo Ancelotti has been named The Best FIFA Men's Coach for 2024.

arts.ttl: “Everyone thought it would be an easy ride” – Real Madrid manager Carlo Ancelotti responds to criticism
arts.sum: Real Madrid have not had an easy season up until now, although they can pick up their second trophy of the campaign if they defeat Pachuca in the Intercontinental Cup on Wednesday. Carlo Ancelotti’s side suffered another disappointing result on Saturday as they were held to a 3-3 draw by Rayo Vallecano.
